stories.headline: IDF's 98th Division gains control over Hezbollah-ridden town in southern Lebanon Per Israeli media citing the IDF's estimates, there are at least several dozen Hezbollah terrorists located in the town, including Radwan Force operatives.
stories.summary: The IDF's 98th Division has gained control over the Hezbollah-ridden town of Bint Jbail in southern Lebanon. Israeli media reports that the town is home to at least several dozen Hezbollah terrorists, including Radwan Force operatives. The division's capture of the town follows a series of military operations in southern Lebanon, where Israeli forces have been targeting Hezbollah strongholds. The IDF has reported destroying 150 Hezbollah targets in the area and capturing a large cache of weaponry and ammunition. The military's efforts aim to remove threats from the terror group towards northern Israel, which has been targeted by Hezbollah rockets. The operation is part of a broader strategic shift by Israel to create "buffer zones" in Gaza, Syria, and Lebanon, following attacks on October 7, 2023.

stories.headline: Benjamin Netanyahu to open direct negotiations with Lebanon on Hezbollah "The negotiations will focus on disarming Hezbollah and establishing peaceful relations between Israel and Lebanon," the prime minister said.
stories.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced that direct negotiations with Lebanon will begin next week in Washington. The talks will focus on disarming Hezbollah and establishing peaceful relations between Israel and Lebanon. Netanyahu stated that Israel would continue to strike Hezbollah with force and precision, despite the upcoming negotiations. The move comes as Iran threatens to not hold negotiations on the war if attacks against Hezbollah in Lebanon continue. An Israeli official confirmed that Israel will not observe a ceasefire in Lebanon during the negotiations. The talks aim to address the long-standing tensions between Israel and Lebanon, with a specific focus on the disarmament of Hezbollah, a militant group based in Lebanon. The negotiations are set to start soon, with US officials facilitating the discussions.

stories.headline: US President Donald Trump’s threat to destroy “a whole civilisation” in Iran has reignited calls to invoke the 25th Amendment and drawn condemnation across the
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has threatened to destroy Iran's "whole civilisation" if Tehran does not comply with his demands, including opening the Strait of Hormuz. Trump warned that Iran's "whole civilisation will die tonight" if a deal is not reached. The threat has drawn condemnation from the UN chief and the Pope, and has reignited calls to invoke the 25th Amendment. Trump has vowed to destroy Iran's power plants and bridges if a deal is not struck, stating that "extortion, corruption and death will end" if Tehran agrees to his terms. The threat has sparked widespread criticism across the political spectrum, with many expressing concern over the potential consequences of such an action. The international community is bracing for a potential escalation of the conflict between the US and Iran.

stories.headline: Zambia and Egypt have reaffirmed their commitment to bilateral cooperation on environmental protection and sustainable development following a high-level meeting
stories.summary: Zambia and Egypt have reaffirmed their commitment to bilateral cooperation on environmental protection and sustainable development. The two countries aim to turn cooperation into tangible outcomes through practical projects, fostering deeper economic ties through joint public-private ventures. Several Memoranda of Understanding were signed to facilitate collaboration in multiple sectors. Egypt's commitment to organizing a high-impact conference on sustainable management of coastal and marine resources was also reaffirmed. This cooperation comes as the global population is expected to reach 10 billion by 2100, increasing the need for sustainable development and environmental protection. Meanwhile, Egypt is also engaging in high-level talks with other countries, including Turkey, Pakistan, Qatar, and Bahrain, to reduce regional tensions and promote cooperation. These efforts demonstrate Egypt's proactive role in addressing global challenges and promoting sustainable development.

stories.headline: AI Plus Launches Nova Flip Phone Priced at Rs 29,999 Alongside Nova 2 and Nova 2 Ultra
stories.summary: AI Plus has launched the Nova Flip phone in India, priced at Rs 29,999, alongside the Nova 2 and Nova 2 Ultra. The Nova Flip is set to launch in May, while the Nova 2 will go on sale starting April 14, and the Nova 2 Ultra on April 17. All devices run on Android 16 with NxtQuantum OS and will be available via Flipkart and select retail outlets. The Nova 2, Nova 2 Ultra, and Nova Flip are part of AI Plus's expanded connected ecosystem, which also includes the PulseTab and new audio and wearable devices. The Nova Flip 5G is one of the most affordable flip smartphones, with a starting price of Rs 29,999. The devices are 5G-enabled, with the Nova 2 and Nova 2 Ultra already unveiled and ready for purchase.

stories.headline: Instagram Expands Its PG-13-Inspired Content Guidelines for Teen Accounts to Europe
stories.summary: Instagram is expanding its revamped Teen Accounts program to Europe, inspired by the PG-13 movie rating criteria and parent feedback. The program restricts the kind of content teen users are exposed to, aiming to show them content similar to what they would see in an age-appropriate movie by default. This expansion is part of Meta's effort to enhance content moderation for younger users on Instagram, introducing a system modeled after the PG-13 cinema rating. The move comes as the social network faces scrutiny over its practices to protect children, following legal cases in New Mexico and Los Angeles. The company's goal is to provide a safer environment for teens, with content restrictions that align with parental expectations and age-based guidelines. The expansion to Europe marks a significant step in Instagram's international efforts to protect its younger users.

stories.headline: Google updates list of best AI models for coding Android apps
stories.summary: Google has updated its "Android Bench" list, a resource for measuring the best AI models for coding Android apps. The list evaluates models based on their ability to work with Jetpack Compose, Coroutines, and other factors. Two new models, OpenAI's GPT 5.4 and GPT 5.3 Codex, have been added to the list and rank highly. Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview remains the top model with a score of 72.4%. The update is part of Google's efforts to improve AI-powered app development, which also includes the launch of the AICore Developer Preview and the Google Antigravity coding agent. These tools aim to make it easier for developers to build and prototype apps using AI models, with features like building multiplayer experiences and connecting to real-world services. The updates reflect Google's ongoing investment in AI-powered app development tools.

stories.headline: All The Biggest Xbox Reveals From The Triple-I Initiative Showcase 2026 Including a new Game Pass announcement!
stories.summary: The Triple-I Initiative Showcase 2026 took place, featuring 40 announcements, including new reveals and updates on announced projects. The showcase highlighted a variety of games across multiple platforms, with many coming to Xbox. A new Game Pass announcement was also made during the event. The Triple-I Initiative is a group of studios that share gaming news without fluff, ads, or hosts, focusing solely on games. The showcase included trailers and reveals from participating studios, providing a comprehensive look at upcoming games. The event has become a premier indie game showcase, and this year's edition did not disappoint, offering a wide range of exciting announcements and updates for gamers to look forward to, particularly Xbox users. The full list of announcements and trailers is available for those who missed the presentation.

stories.headline: Netball: ANZ Premiership at tipping point after decade Analysis: It should be celebrating a milestone 10th season. Instead, officials are working furiously to secure its future.
stories.summary: The ANZ Premiership is at a critical juncture as it approaches its 10th season in 2026. Despite marking a decade of elite domestic netball, officials are scrambling to secure the competition's future. The league has experienced a decade of growth, with teams like the Mainland Tactix consistently competing at a high level. However, the Tactix have fallen short in the grand final for two consecutive seasons. As the ANZ Premiership enters a new era, its core players, including Tactix captain Jane Watson, are expected to return and compete again. The league's future is uncertain, prompting officials to work diligently to ensure its continuation beyond the milestone 10th season. The upcoming season will be crucial in determining the direction of the ANZ Premiership and its place in the netball landscape.

stories.headline: Kansas City unveils $600M plan for Royals downtown stadium The prospect of downtown baseball is a step closer to reality.
stories.summary: The City of Kansas City has introduced a $600 million funding package to build a new downtown stadium for the Kansas City Royals baseball team. The proposed 'Downtown Baseball District' would invest $1.9 billion into the area around Washington. The ordinance, introduced by Mayor Quinton Lucas, would create a framework for the project if passed by the City Council. The new stadium would be home to the Royals when their lease at Kauffman Stadium expires after the 2030 season. The funding package aims to keep the team on the Missouri side of the state line. The proposed development would transform the area, bringing new investment and activity to downtown Kansas City. The City Council's approval is needed to move the project forward, bringing downtown baseball one step closer to reality.

stories.headline: Otherworldly McDavid helps Oilers bolster lead in Pacific Division Between Connor McDavid's five-point night and their shutdown defensive play, the Oilers were in total command of Wednesday's 5-2 win over the Sharks.
stories.summary: The Edmonton Oilers bolstered their lead in the Pacific Division with recent wins. Connor McDavid had a five-point night in a 5-2 win over the San Jose Sharks on Wednesday, showcasing the team's shutdown defensive play. The Oilers then achieved their first five-game win streak of the season with a 3-1 victory over the Chicago Blackhawks on Thursday, tying them with the Anaheim Ducks for top spot in the Pacific Division. In their next game, McDavid had four assists as the Oilers secured a playoff spot with a 4-2 win over the Sharks on Friday. This win solidified the Oilers' position in the division, currently sitting in a strong position to compete for the top spot. The team's performance has been impressive, with McDavid's outstanding play being a key factor.

stories.headline: Soccer fans give 'stupid, stupid' 2026 World Cup jerseys the cold shoulder Fans love the designs of Nike's new World Cup soccer jerseys. They do not love the way they bunch at the shoulders.
stories.summary: Soccer fans are expressing disappointment with Nike's new 2026 World Cup jerseys due to a design flaw. The issue lies in the shoulder area, where the seams cause the fabric to bunch up, creating an unsightly bulge. Fans have taken to social media to voice their frustration, with many stating that the problem is exacerbated for those with wider shoulders. The jerseys, which cost between $100 to $200, have been criticized for poor design and quality. Despite liking the overall design of the jerseys, fans are unhappy with the shoulder issue, which they believe should have been caught before production. The problem was first noticed when players wore the kits for the first time during the international break, and since then, fans have been sharing their discontent online.

stories.headline: Over 90 lakh voters excluded in West Bengal, with significant disparities in deletion rates between Hindus and Muslims.
stories.summary: Nearly 91 lakh voters have been deleted from West Bengal's electoral rolls following a revision by the Election Commission of India, cutting over 11% of the electorate. The Special Intensive Revision exercise removed around 63.66 lakh voters, about 8.3% of the state's total electorate, since the process began. A further 2,716,393 individuals were found not eligible out of 6,006,675 flagged for review. The deletion rates show significant disparities between Hindus and Muslims, with Muslim-majority Murshidabad district having the maximum number of deleted voters. The Election Commission released the data on February 28, sparking anxiety ahead of polls. The district-wise list of eligible and ineligible voters revealed that 27,16,393 voters were found ineligible out of 60.06 lakh marked under adjudication. The large-scale exclusion has raised concerns about the impact on the upcoming elections.

stories.headline: The police in Andhra Pradesh arrested a 38-year-old single mother, who allegedly recruited 42 women through IS-linked online groups.
stories.summary: Andhra Pradesh police arrested a 38-year-old single mother for allegedly recruiting 42 women through IS-linked online groups. The arrest is part of a larger operation to bust an ISIS-linked terror module with a pan-India network and foreign handlers. The police had earlier deployed teams across several states, including Bihar, Delhi, and Karnataka, to arrest over a dozen suspects linked to the network. In a separate incident, a 32-year-old woman was allegedly raped and assaulted by an unidentified person in Macherla. The police are investigating both cases, with the Palnadu SP confirming the woman's assault and the East Godavari district police arresting a fraudster who cheated women through online matrimony sites. The arrests and investigations highlight the police's efforts to combat terrorism and crime in the region.

stories.headline: Fire breaks out at Mumbai airport’s Terminal 1 Teams from the Mumbai Fire Brigade, along with police and ward staff, rushed to the spot and initiated firefighting operations.
stories.summary: A fire broke out at Terminal 1 of Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport in Mumbai on Thursday evening. The blaze originated on the ground floor and was reported in the cable trays, switch boxes, and parts of the seating area inside Terminal 1B. Teams from the Mumbai Fire Brigade, police, and ward staff rushed to the spot and initiated firefighting operations. The fire was reportedly caused by an electrical issue and was quickly contained with no reported injuries. Airport operations remain unaffected. The swift response from emergency services helped to douse the fire, and the situation was brought under control. The Mumbai Fire Department and other authorities responded promptly to the incident, ensuring minimal disruption to airport activities. The cause of the fire is attributed to an electrical issue, according to initial reports.

stories.headline: Global Ayyappa Sangamam: Kerala HC flags lapses in Devaswom finances, bats for centralised digital system
stories.summary: The Kerala High Court identified discrepancies in the financial management of the Travancore Devaswom Board (TDB) related to the 'Global Ayyappa Sangamam' event. The court flagged systemic deficiencies and issued directions to improve the board's financial system, ensuring transparency and accountability. Alleged discrepancies in the audit of the event were reported, prompting the court to seek explanations from the TDB. The court criticized financial irregularities, including tender bypass and audit gaps, in the event's accounts. It directed the auditor to detail past lapses and propose reforms for temple fund accountability. The next hearing is scheduled for April 1. The court's actions aim to address the shortcomings in the TDB's financial management and ensure a more transparent and accountable system for managing temple funds. The TDB is required to provide a detailed explanation and implement reforms to prevent such irregularities in the future.

stories.headline: Bitcoin holds near $71K as markets weigh geopolitics and macro signals
stories.summary: Bitcoin's price holds near $71,000 as markets consider geopolitical tensions and macroeconomic signals. Ethereum and XRP exhibit mixed but stable capital flows. Geopolitical tensions, particularly in the Middle East, remain a key factor in determining the near-term direction of cryptocurrencies. The funding rate is at 6%, indicating a potential sharp rally above $75,000. Despite this, the price steadies after an earlier drop below $70,000, with traders weighing persistent tensions against signs of diplomatic engagement between Washington and Tehran. Bitcoin edged 1% higher to $71,197.8, with institutional sentiment mixed and ETF flows alternating between inflows and outflows. The cryptocurrency's stability near $71,000 suggests a potential breakthrough, but ongoing tensions and macro signals will continue to influence its direction.

stories.headline: Economists see prolonged rate pause; hike risks hinge on oil, geopolitics
stories.summary: Economists expect a prolonged pause in interest rate hikes, with future movements dependent on global events and domestic inflation trends. The Reserve Bank of India is likely to maintain steady interest rates, focusing on financial stability and managing global risks. Inflation projections are within tolerance levels, and no immediate policy tightening is expected. Similarly, the US Federal Reserve is predicted to keep interest rates on hold until September, with some economists still expecting a rate reduction later this year. However, rising inflation fears have led to speculation about a potential rate hike, which Fed Chair Powell has warned could be counterproductive if done in response to an oil-driven spike in inflation. The timing of any rate changes will be crucial, as monetary policy has long and variable lags, potentially taking effect after the energy shock subsides.

stories.headline: Commodity boom now driven by de-dollarisation and geopolitics
stories.summary: Commodity markets are experiencing a boom driven by de-dollarisation and geopolitics, signaling a new supercycle. The Bloomberg Commodity Index (BCOM) has surged ahead of its rival, the UBS Bloomberg Constant Maturity Commodity Index (CMCITR), marking a significant divergence. This outperformance is attributed to differences in index construction. Commodities trading at CME Group has reached record volumes in 2025, with structural shifts such as geopolitical tensions and trade policies reshaping global markets. The energy transition and de-dollarisation are also key drivers, with experts predicting a significant impact on gold, energy, and metals markets. The New QFI route has enabled commodity traders to manage risk and access Chinese commodity futures prices without setting up a local entity, allowing for true arbitrage opportunities. This shift is expected to continue, driven by fundamental changes in global trade and markets.

stories.headline: Why Trump’s fondness for Munir runs deeper than geopolitics
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has held two high-profile meetings with Pakistan's Field Marshal Asim Munir at the White House in three months, sparking interest in their growing relationship. Analysts suggest Trump's praise for Munir stems from psychological needs, with Munir offering validation and mirroring Trump's admiration for strong leaders. This relationship prioritizes mutual ego gratification and transactional benefits over traditional diplomacy. Munir has driven Pakistan's geopolitical reemergence, and his growing role in US-Pakistan relations marks a significant turn in his fortunes, despite controversies at home. The meetings have raised questions about the motives behind their bonhomie, highlighting Trump's preference for personalized loyalty over institutional alliances, which shapes his foreign policy decisions and may impact US-Pakistan relations and regional stability. Munir's emergence as Trump's "favourite field marshal" is a notable development in South Asian geopolitics.

stories.headline: Why stock market is rising today? Sensex surges 500 pts, Nifty above 23,900. 3 key reasons behind Rs 4 lakh crore gains Indian stock markets surged on Friday, with Sensex and Nifty climbing approximately 0.7%.
stories.summary: Indian stock markets surged on Friday, with Sensex rising 500 points and Nifty above 23,900, resulting in Rs 4 lakh crore gains. The rally is attributed to three key factors: global market recovery, easing US-Iran tensions, and falling crude oil prices. The Sensex and Nifty climbed approximately 0.7% and nearly 2% respectively, with IT and metal sectors leading the gains. Nifty Realty and Nifty Auto led the gains, rallying nearly 7% each, while Nifty PSU Bank, Nifty Consumer Durables, and Nifty Private Bank rose over 5%. The strengthening of the rupee also contributed to the market's positive sentiment. The global risk-on rally, triggered by easing tensions and falling crude oil prices, boosted investor sentiment, leading to the surge in the Indian stock markets.

stories.headline: Sensex tanks 1,100 points, Nifty cracks 1%: Why is the stock market falling today? 6 reasons explained Sensex crashes 1,100 pts, Nifty falls 1% on global worries, profit booking, and rising oil—read why the market is down today!
stories.summary: The Indian stock market plummeted on Friday, with the Sensex crashing 1,100 points and the Nifty falling 1%. The decline was driven by global worries, profit booking, and rising oil prices. Geopolitical tensions involving Israel and Iran led to a 13% spike in Brent crude prices, contributing to the market downturn. Additionally, US-Iran tensions and FII selling also played a role in the decline. On the National Stock Exchange, 597 stocks declined, while 1,969 advanced and 76 remained unchanged. The Nifty Smallcap 100 and Nifty Midcap 100 indices were the only gainers, with marginal increases. The sharp decline came after a strong rally in the previous session, with the BSE Sensex falling 900 points intraday and the Nifty slipping below 23,800. The surge in oil prices and global tensions weighed heavily on the market.

stories.headline: Market fall today: Sensex tanks 900 pts as US-Iran tensions flare up again Stock Market Crash Today: BSE Sensex fell 900 pts intraday and Nifty slipped below 23,800 amid US-Iran tensions, oil surge, and FII selling.
stories.summary: The BSE Sensex fell 931.25 points to 76,631.65, a 1.2% decline, while the Nifty declined 222.25 points to 23,775.10, a 0.93% drop. The market downturn was attributed to US-Iran tensions and a surge in oil prices, as well as selling by foreign institutional investors. The Sensex had tanked over 900 points intraday, with the Nifty slipping below 23,800. The decline marked a reversal from the previous session's sharp rally. Investors remained wary due to geopolitical uncertainty, particularly Iran's threat to exit talks if Israel continued bombing Lebanon, which could impact the Strait of Hormuz. The Indian equity market snapped a 5-day gaining streak, with the Sensex and Nifty ending lower on Thursday.

stories.headline: Stock market today (April 9, 2026): Which are the top gainers and losers in Nifty50 and BSE Sensex today? Check list India Business News: Equity benchmark indices closed lower on Thursday, with the Sensex tumbling 931 points after the previous session’s sharp rally, as renew..
stories.summary: Equity benchmark indices closed lower on Thursday, with the Sensex declining 931.25 points or 1.20% to 76,631.65. The Nifty 50 also fell, closing at 22,399.15, down 0.61%. The decline was attributed to profit booking and weak global cues, as well as foreign fund outflows. Despite the overall downturn, some stocks gained, with Hindalco emerging as the top gainer in the Nifty50 index, rising 3.56%. Other notable gainers included Dr. Reddy's, Bajaj Auto, BEL, JSW Steel, and Nestle India. The market's sharp decline followed a sharp rebound on Monday, where the Sensex jumped 787.30 points, supported by a correction in crude oil prices and strong buying in banking stocks. The market's volatility has been marked by significant fluctuations in recent days.

stories.headline: USPS to suspend pension contributions, seeks 4-cent stamp price hike
stories.summary: The US Postal Service will temporarily suspend its employer contributions to the Federal Employees Retirement System annuities. This move aims to conserve cash as the organization faces a financial crisis, having lost $9 billion in 2025. The suspension is expected to help the Postal Service offset its cash shortage, as it warns of potentially running out of money within 12 months. Additionally, the Postal Service is seeking a 4-cent price hike for first-class mail Forever stamps, which would increase the price from 78 cents to 82 cents if approved by regulators. This decision is part of the organization's efforts to address its financial struggles and ensure its continued operation. The temporary suspension of pension contributions and potential price increase are intended to help the Postal Service navigate its current financial challenges.

stories.headline: IMF flags rising risks as $4trn nonbank flows reshape emerging
stories.summary: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has warned that emerging economies are at greater risk of higher interest rates and currency shocks due to increased reliance on nonbank financial investors, such as hedge funds. Portfolio inflows to emerging markets have risen eightfold since the 2008 global financial crisis, reaching $4 trillion. The IMF notes that these investors' flows are highly sensitive to shifts in global risk sentiment, making emerging markets more vulnerable to external shocks. The ongoing war in the Middle East has already triggered capital flow reversals in some emerging markets, highlighting the risks. The IMF cautions that nonbank financial investors, while expanding funding access for emerging markets, also expose them to greater risks, including those related to the Iran war. This increased vulnerability poses significant challenges for emerging economies.

stories.headline: OpenAI pauses UK investment deal over energy costs and regulation
stories.summary: OpenAI is pausing its Stargate artificial intelligence infrastructure project in the UK due to regulatory concerns and high energy costs. The company will continue to explore the project and move forward when conditions enable long-term infrastructure investment. Britain's energy prices, already significantly higher than in the US, have been further impacted by the Iran war. The UK's regulatory approach to AI has also been a concern for OpenAI. The pause deals a blow to the UK government's efforts to position the country as a global AI hub. OpenAI, the maker of ChatGPT, had been investing in the UK, but the unfavourable regulatory environment and high energy costs have led to the temporary halt of the project. The company will reassess and potentially restart the project when conditions improve.

stories.headline: Mark Zuckerberg announces Muse Spark, a new Meta AI model: How to try it, benchmark results
stories.summary: Meta has unveiled Muse Spark, a new flagship artificial intelligence model, its first under the revamped AI division. Mark Zuckerberg announced the model, which is purpose-built for Meta's products, including Instagram, Facebook, and Threads. Muse Spark is designed to power a smarter and faster Meta AI, unlocking new features such as recommendations and content sharing. The model is Meta's most powerful yet, with larger models currently in development. Benchmark results show promising performance, potentially putting Meta back in the AI race after its previous model, Llama 4. The Muse Spark model is led by chief AI officer Alexandr Wang, who heads Meta Superintelligence Labs. The model's performance will be tested by independent experts to confirm its capabilities. Muse Spark is the first in a new family of AI models, marking a significant development in Meta's AI division.

stories.headline: Florida AG opens probe into OpenAI ahead of potential IPO
stories.summary: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier has launched an investigation into OpenAI and its chatbot ChatGPT, citing concerns over national security, public safety, and potential harm to minors. The probe comes as OpenAI prepares for a potential initial public offering (IPO) that could value the company at up to $1 trillion. Uthmeier expressed concerns that AI could lead to an existential crisis or harm humanity. The investigation will also examine the company's potential link to a shooting at Florida State and alleged gaps in AI regulation. Subpoenas are expected to be issued as part of the investigation, which aims to address concerns over the safety and security implications of OpenAI's technology. The probe highlights growing regulatory scrutiny of AI companies as they expand their reach and influence.

stories.headline: OpenAI pulls out of landmark £31bn UK investment package
stories.summary: OpenAI has put on hold its landmark UK investment project, citing high energy costs and an unfavourable regulatory environment. The project was part of a £31bn package of UK tech investments, aimed at supporting the country's "AI Growth Zone" and creating 5,000 new jobs. The suspended Stargate UK project was expected to bring in £30bn in private investment. Other companies, including Microsoft and Nvidia, have made similar multibillion-dollar investment commitments in the UK. The delay deals a blow to the UK government's efforts to position the country as an "AI superpower". OpenAI's decision to pause its main data centre project in Britain is a significant setback for the government's plans to boost the UK's tech industry. The project's suspension is attributed to the high energy costs and regulatory hurdles in the country.

stories.headline: OpenAI Pauses Stargate Data Center Project in UK
stories.summary: OpenAI has paused its Stargate data center project in the UK due to high energy costs and regulatory issues. The project, announced in September, aimed to expand the UK's "sovereign compute capabilities" and support native AI development. However, industrial electricity prices in the UK are four times higher than in the US, making the project less viable. Additionally, unresolved AI copyright rules have contributed to the pause. The Stargate project is part of OpenAI's plan to increase global data center capacity to run and train its AI models, with similar projects underway in the US, Norway, and the United Arab Emirates. The pause deals a blow to the UK government's efforts to establish the country as a global AI hub. OpenAI's decision highlights the challenges of developing large-scale AI infrastructure in the UK.

stories.headline: From OpenAI to Nvidia, tech firms channel billions into AI infrastructure as demand booms
stories.summary: Tech giants are investing heavily in artificial intelligence infrastructure, with CoreWeave and Meta expanding their cloud capacity agreement to $21 billion. OpenAI is securing significant funding and partnerships with companies like Amazon, Disney, and Nvidia. Amazon has invested $4 billion in Anthropic, an OpenAI competitor, while SoftBank is acquiring DigitalBridge Group in a $4 billion deal to expand its AI-related portfolio. OpenAI has partnered with Broadcom to produce its first in-house AI processors, amid surging demand for AI infrastructure. Nvidia is also investing heavily in Anthropic and acquiring other AI-related companies. These investments demonstrate the growing demand for AI infrastructure, with global industry giants pouring hundreds of billions into AI chips and cloud capacity. Meta is also forging deals with AMD, Manus, CoreWeave, Oracle, and Google to support its AI infrastructure.
